faqs.title: Healthcare marketing services — FAQ
faqs.indexIntro: How scope, timelines, ad budgets, compliance, and engagement model actually work when you bring on a healthcare growth partner - written for the people doing the hiring.
faqs.stillCardP1: Multi-location group, brand-new launch, or an existing agency you're ready to replace - we'll size the engagement to your stage. No template proposals.
faqs.stillCardP2: One call. We'll audit your current funnel, name the three biggest revenue leaks, and tell you which services would actually move them.

items.q: What healthcare marketing services do you actually deliver?
items.a: We run a full healthcare growth stack under one roof - local SEO, Google Business Profile optimization, Google & Meta paid media, content + schema, conversion-focused website builds, review and reputation engines, and HIPAA-aware tracking. On top of that, we ship custom software and clinical automation / AI workflows when your growth bottleneck is operational, not marketing. Most clients engage us as their outsourced growth team rather than a single-channel vendor.

items.q: How long does SEO take to grow patient bookings?
items.a: Local Pack and Google Business Profile wins typically show in the first 30–60 days - category accuracy, photo cadence, review velocity, NAP consistency, and on-page schema fixes move fast. Organic search rankings for high-intent service keywords compound through months 3–6, and the long-tail content / authority work lifts traffic across months 6–12. Paid media bridges the gap from day one so you have booked appointments while organic catches up.

items.q: Should we run Google Ads, Meta Ads, or both?
items.a: It depends on intent. Google Ads captures patients already searching (urgent care, ER, emergency dental, "near me" terms) - high CPC but high intent. Meta & Instagram Ads create demand for elective and aesthetic services (MedSpa, plastic surgery, orthodontics) where the patient hasn't typed a search query yet. Most multi-service clinics run both, weighted by service line. We rebuild your tracking with server-side conversions and offline conversion uploads so spend gets attributed to actual booked revenue, not just form fills.

items.q: What custom software, automations, and AI workflows can you build for a clinic?
items.a: Anything that lives between your marketing engine and your clinical ops. Common builds: AI patient triage chatbots, intake form automations, EHR / practice-management API integrations, automated recall and no-show recovery sequences, real-time reporting dashboards, and HIPAA-compliant patient portals. We deploy these on top of your existing stack (Athena, eClinicalWorks, NextGen, Epic, OpenDental, etc.) so the front desk runs lighter and marketing data flows back into the EHR cleanly.

items.q: How do you keep tracking, retargeting, and ad creative HIPAA-compliant?
items.a: We rebuilt our tracking stack after Meta's 2023 healthcare audit and the FTC's pixel guidance. Standard kit: server-side GTM, hashed conversions, IP redaction, BAA-covered vendors, custom-event-only retargeting (never PHI-based audiences), and clinical-claim review on every paid ad. We also stand up consent banners and explicit opt-ins where state telehealth or therapy rules require them. If a vendor in your funnel can't sign a BAA, we route around it.

items.q: Do you charge per service or as a bundled monthly retainer?
items.a: Mostly monthly retainers - that's how compounding channels (SEO, content, review velocity, retargeting audiences) actually pay back. Retainers are scoped by service mix and ad-spend tier, with a flat strategy fee on top so the agency makes money on outcomes, not ad-buying volume. One-off engagements (website rebuilds, custom software, automation projects, audit-only engagements) are quoted as fixed-fee projects. We share pricing on the discovery call once we've seen your funnel.

faqs.q: How long does a new clinic site take?
faqs.a: Design plus build runs 6 to 9 weeks for single-location practices, 10 to 14 for multi-location systems. We sequence content collection up front so the build never stalls waiting on copy.

faqs.q: Will the new site be HIPAA-compliant?
faqs.a: Yes. Every site we ship runs on BAA-covered hosting with encrypted forms, audit-friendly logging, and zero third-party pixels on pages that touch PHI. We hand you a HIPAA-architecture diagram at launch.

faqs.q: Can the booking widget connect to our EHR?
faqs.a: Most modern EHRs (Athena, eClinicalWorks, NextGen, Kareo, Cerner, Epic via integration partners) expose schedule APIs we can wire to. We also support widget-style providers (Solv, Zocdoc) when direct API isn't available.

faqs.q: Does it have to be in WordPress?
faqs.a: No. We default to a modern Next.js stack for new builds because it's faster, more secure, and easier to update. WordPress is fine when the team needs to ship blog posts daily; we still harden it for HIPAA.

faqs.q: What about Core Web Vitals and SEO impact?
faqs.a: Every new site ships with green-band LCP, INP, and CLS, structured data on every service and location, and a redirect map so legacy URLs don't lose authority. We measure SEO impact monthly post-launch.

faqs.q: How do you handle ADA / WCAG compliance?
faqs.a: We run automated axe / Lighthouse checks throughout the build and a manual screen-reader pass before launch. Every site ships with a written WCAG 2.2 AA audit report you can hand to legal if a demand letter arrives.

faqs.q: Will the redesign hurt our existing rankings?
faqs.a: Not if it is done right. We map every legacy URL to its new destination with 301s, preserve title / meta / heading equity on top pages, and re-submit the sitemap. Most sites we relaunch hold or gain rank within 60 days.

faqs.q: Who owns the site after launch?
faqs.a: You do. Code, design files, hosting account, domain - all in your name from day one. We can manage hosting and updates as a retainer, or hand off cleanly.
